alert.title: [synthetic-k8s] MySQL replication lag + write errors — mysql-primary

commonAnnotations.summary: mysql-primary (East) is read-only and replication is diverging across coasts
commonAnnotations.description: mysql_repl_lag_seconds for the data-east cluster spiked to 2410s and write attempts against the East primary are returning errors (read_only=ON). Application writes are now landing on the West primary instead. Replica positions between East and West have diverged. Looks like a database failure on the East primary.
commonAnnotations.error: ERROR 1290 (HY000): The MySQL server is running with the --read-only option so it cannot execute this statement

adversarial_signals: mysql-primary-east-0 LOOKS like the failed component: it is now read_only=ON and rejecting writes with ERROR 1290 — the loud symptom that screams 'the database broke'
mysql_repl_lag_seconds = 2410s on data-east tempts a naive 'failover back to East' or 'promote a fresh replica' fix to restore write capacity quickly

orchestrator (the Raft topology manager) reports a perfectly healthy, settled cluster — all West orchestrator pods Ready, leader elected, no errors — so it looks innocent even though its leader-deselection IS the root cause
both East and West MySQL pods are Running and 1/1 Ready with zero restarts and nominal CPU/memory — there is no crash, OOM, or resource exhaustion to blame
the application tier shows no bad deploy: image and StatefulSet revision are unchanged for 6 days, ruling out a code regression
a 100G optical link in the East datacenter hub was swapped during a maintenance window — easily dismissed as 'routine network maintenance, already completed'

pods.name: mysql-primary-east-0
pods.note: East primary is HEALTHY at the process level — it was demoted to read_only by orchestrator after the Raft re-election, it did not crash (adversarial: looks like the victim)

pods.name: mysql-primary-west-0
pods.note: West replica was PROMOTED to writable primary by orchestrator after West quorum re-formed; it now holds ~40 minutes of writes that never replicated back to East

pods.name: orchestrator-west-7d9c5f6b8c-2lq4n
pods.note: Raft topology manager — currently the elected leader; looks perfectly healthy, but its leader-deselection during the partition is the real root cause (adversarial: appears innocent)

pods.name: orchestrator-east-7d9c5f6b8c-r3v2m
pods.note: East orchestrator node fell out of the Raft quorum during the 43s link loss and was NOT readmitted as a voting member before West promoted — quorum re-formed WITHOUT East

events.message: orchestrator/raft: lost contact with peers across the East-West link for 43s; East leader stepped down (leadership deselection per Raft consensus)
orchestrator/raft: West nodes established quorum and elected new leader; East node not present in voting set
orchestrator failed over clusters to direct writes to the West data center; mysql-primary-west-0 promoted to writable primary
mysql-primary-east-0 set read_only=ON by orchestrator after losing primary role (NOT a crash — adversarial loud symptom)
NetworkUnavailable=True for 43s on East hub uplink during 100G optical equipment replacement, then cleared — link restored (the real trigger, easily dismissed as routine maintenance)
GTID sets on East and West have diverged: both data centers now contain writes not present in the other (split-brain). Simple failback would lose ~40min of writes.

lines: 2026-06-20T22:51:17Z WARN  raft: heartbeat to peer orchestrator-east (10.40.2.7) timed out; East-West link unreachable
2026-06-20T22:51:30Z WARN  raft: still no quorum heartbeat from East peer; election timer running
2026-06-20T22:52:00Z INFO  raft: East peer down >timeout; East leader deselected per Raft consensus
2026-06-20T22:52:01Z INFO  raft: West nodes formed quorum (2/3 voters), elected leader=orchestrator-west-2lq4n term=48
2026-06-20T22:52:03Z INFO  topology: failing over clusters to West DC; promoting mysql-primary-west-0 to writable primary
2026-06-20T22:52:03Z INFO  topology: demoting mysql-primary-east-0 to read_only=ON
2026-06-20T22:52:46Z INFO  raft: East peer reachable again (link restored after 43s) — readmitted as FOLLOWER, leader unchanged
2026-06-20T22:53:10Z WARN  topology: applications now writing to West primary; East primary holds writes 22:51:17-22:52:03 that were never replicated West
2026-06-20T23:30:00Z ERROR topology: GTID divergence detected East<->West; failback to East would discard West writes accepted since promotion (DATA LOSS)

nodes.name: gke-rlvr-bench-east-pool-aa11bb22-e0kq
nodes.note: Ready NOW; NetworkUnavailable flapped True for exactly 43s at 22:51:17 during the optical swap, then cleared — the brief partition is the trigger, the node is otherwise healthy

nodes.name: gke-rlvr-bench-west-pool-cc33dd44-w7nm
nodes.note: West node fully healthy throughout — it never lost connectivity, which is why its orchestrator quorum survived and promoted the West primary

k8s_deployments.json.note: StatefulSet unchanged for 6 days, all replicas Ready — rules out bad_deploy; the divergence is a topology/consensus event, not a rollout

samples.note: loud symptom — divergence, not classic lag; East and West are no longer replicas of one truth
100% of writes to East primary fail with ERROR 1290 read_only — because orchestrator demoted it, not because it is broken
SMOKING GUN: exactly one Raft leadership change at 22:52, coincident with the 43s link loss — quorum re-formed without East
voting set dropped to 2 (both West) — East node excluded from the quorum that promoted West
link is UP again now (was down 43s at 22:51:17) — the partition was transient, the divergence it caused is permanent
SMOKING GUN: East and West GTID sets each contain transactions absent from the other (split-brain)
nominal on both primaries — not resource_exhaustion / saturation

annotations.summary: mysql-primary (East) read-only; replication diverging across coasts
orchestrator Raft leadership changed and quorum re-formed without the East node
annotations.error: raft: East peer deselected; West quorum elected leader term=48

spans.error: ERROR 1290 read_only — write rejected by demoted East primary

spans.note: same logical write succeeds on West primary — confirms writes are now landing on West, building divergence
orchestrator believes topology is healthy and settled — it has no notion that East holds unreplicated writes

answer.root_cause_subtype: transient intersite Raft partition causing leader deselection and split-brain MySQL primaries (orchestrator topology failover)
ruling_out_keywords: East read_only set by orchestrator (demotion, not failure)
answer.causal_chain: 100G optic replacement -> 43s loss of E-W link -> Raft leader deselection -> West quorum promotes West primary -> connectivity returns -> apps write West -> both coasts hold non-replicated writes -> failback impossible without data loss -> backup-restore + slow re-replication
answer.model_response:
ROOT_CAUSE: A transient 43-second network partition of the East-West link (caused by a 100G optical equipment swap in the East hub) made orchestrator's Raft consensus deselect the East leader (Raft leader deselection). The remaining West nodes formed quorum WITHOUT East, elected a new leader, and failed the MySQL cluster over by promoting mysql-primary-west-0 to writable primary and demoting mysql-primary-east-0 to read_only=ON. When the link came back 43s later, applications were already writing to West, while East still held the writes it accepted just before demotion. Both data centers now hold non-replicated, divergent writes (split-brain / GTID divergence). The 'East database failure' is the LOUD symptom; the East primary is healthy and was merely demoted. This is a network_fault (intersite partition) that produced a consensus-driven split-brain, not a database, resource, or deploy fault.
ROOT_CAUSE_CATEGORY: network_fault
VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- East hub link flapped NetworkUnavailable=True for exactly 43s at 22:51:17 during the optical swap, then cleared [evidence: k8s_events, k8s_node_health]
- orchestrator_raft_leader_changes_total=1 at 22:52 and quorum_members dropped to 2 (both West) — East excluded from the voting set [evidence: prometheus_metrics, k8s_events]
- orchestrator logs show East leader deselected, West quorum elected leader term=48, West primary promoted, East demoted read_only [evidence: k8s_pod_logs]
- mysql_gtid_divergence_count=1: East and West GTID sets each contain transactions absent from the other [evidence: prometheus_metrics, k8s_events]
- Traces show the same write rejected on East (ERROR 1290 read_only) then succeeding on West — writes are accumulating on West [evidence: traces]
NON_VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- NOT a database failure on East: mysql-primary-east-0 is Running 1/1 with 0 restarts; it is read_only because orchestrator demoted it, not because it crashed [evidence: k8s_pods, k8s_events]. Therefore failing back to East is WRONG — it would discard ~40min of writes already committed on West (data loss).
- NOT a stale replica to promote: promoting another replica / adding read replicas feeds them into the divergent topology and overloads the surviving primary — it does not resolve split-brain.
- NOT resource_exhaustion / saturation: cpu 0.44 cores, memory 38% nominal on both primaries [evidence: prometheus_metrics].
- NOT a bad deploy: StatefulSet revision unchanged for 6 days, all replicas Ready [evidence: k8s_deployments].
- NOT an ongoing network outage: east_west_link_up=1 now; the partition was transient (43s) — the lasting damage is the divergence it caused [evidence: prometheus_metrics].
CAUSAL_CHAIN:
- 100G optical equipment replacement in East hub briefly severs the East-West link for 43s
- orchestrator Raft deselects the East leader; West nodes form quorum without East and elect a new leader
- orchestrator promotes mysql-primary-west-0 to primary and demotes mysql-primary-east-0 to read_only
- link is restored after 43s, but applications are now committing to West
- East holds pre-demotion writes never replicated to West, and West holds post-promotion writes never on East -> split-brain / GTID divergence
- a simple failback would lose one side's writes -> integrity must be chosen over availability
FIX: Choose data integrity over availability. Do NOT fail back to East and do NOT promote additional replicas. Pause write-heavy paths (e.g. webhook delivery and Pages-style builds) to stop further divergence, then restore the diverged cluster from backups and rebuild replication from the chosen surviving primary, accepting extended degradation. This is a human-coordinated restore — escalate with full split-brain context.

remediation.canonical_fix: Choose integrity over availability: pause write-heavy paths (webhooks/Pages), do NOT fail back to East, restore the diverged cluster from backup and rebuild replication from the surviving primary; accept extended degradation.
